title: Chaturmukha Basadi
tagline: Chaturmukha Basadi — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: Chaturmukha Basadi is a symmetrical Jain temple situated in Karkala, Karnataka, India.
overview.description: Chaturmukha Basadi is a symmetrical Jain temple situated in Karkala, Karnataka, India. It is one of the most famous monuments in Karkala.

topPlaces.name: Gommateshwara statue, Karkala
topPlaces.description: Gommateshwara statue, Karkala (ಗೊಮ್ಮಟೇಶ್ವರ ಪ್ರತಿಮೆ ಕಾರ್ಕಳ) is located at Karkala in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is the second tallest statue of Bahubali in the world with the largest statue located at Shravanabelagola.

topPlaces.name: Yennehole
topPlaces.description: Yennehole is a small village near Ajekar in Karkala taluk of Udupi district in India. There is a small river, Swarna, running through this village by same name.

topPlaces.name: Sri Venkataramana Temple, Karkala
topPlaces.description: Sri Venkataramana Temple, Karkala, also known as Padutirupathi, is a temple in Karnataka, India. It was built by Goud Saraswat Brahmins of Karkala and was installed on 25 April 1537.

topPlaces.name: Karkala
topPlaces.description: Karkala (Kannada: [kɑːɾkəɭə]) (Tulu: [kɑːɾlə]) is a town and the headquarters of eponymous Karkala taluk in the Udupi district of Karnataka state in India. It is well known as an important center of Jainism, with several ancient basadis (Jain temples) and the iconic 42-foot monolithic statue of Bahubali (Gommateshwara) dating back to the 15th century.

topPlaces.name: Sanoor
topPlaces.description: Sanoor is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. During the British Raj, the village was headed by the Patels (Patler).

topPlaces.name: Miyar
topPlaces.description: Miyar or Miyyaru is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Karkala taluk of Udupi district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Nitte
topPlaces.description: Nitte is a village in Karkala Taluk, Udupi District, Karnataka, India. It is located on the way from Padubidri to Karkala, about 26 kilometres southeast of the district capital Udupi, and 7 kilometres southwest of the taluk capital Karkala.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chaturmukha Basadi?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chaturmukha Basadi is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chaturmukha Basadi?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (30 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (42 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chaturmukha Basadi?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chaturmukha Basadi at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chaturmukha Basadi?
faq.a: Stays in Chaturmukha Basadi start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chaturmukha Basadi?
faq.a: The top attractions are Gommateshwara statue, Karkala, Yennehole, Sri Venkataramana Temple, Karkala, Karkala, St. Lawrence Shrine Basilica.
